name: Cowboy Pintos
recipeIngredient: 1 large onion (peeled), cut into half-inch dice
4 cloves garlic, peeled and finely minced
2 ribs celery, cut into half-inch dice
1 28-oz. can plum tomatoes (reserve juices), drained and chopped
2 large fresh cilantro sprigs, lightly bruised
freshly ground black pepper to taste
cooked white rice or barley (for serving)
recipeInstructions:
1. Pick over the beans and discard any stones.
2. Soak overnight in water covered with 2 inches of water.
3. Rinse several times and drain.
4. Place oil in a large, heavy pot over medium-low heat.
5. Add onion and cook for 10 minutes or until wilted. Stir in garlic during the last 2 minutes.
6. Add carrots, celery, and drained beans.
7. Add chopped tomatoes with half of the reserved juices, water, thyme, oregano, and cilantro sprigs.
8. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to medium, and simmer uncovered for about 1 1/2 hours or until beans are tender but not mushy. Skim any foam that rises to the surface.
9. Stir in chopped cilantro and season with salt and pepper to taste.
10. Serve immediately as a soup or serve over cooked white rice or barley. Makes 6 servings.
